notes: Government/EGAT-owned course open to the public. Green fees include caddie: 560 THB weekday / 760 THB weekend per golfdd.com (2023). Caddie required and included in fee. No cart hire available. No driving range. Possible duplicate with ubonrat-golf-course (same phone, 0.1 km apart on AllSquare) — treating as the primary/canonical listing as it has more source coverage and confirmed GPS. GPS 16.7547/102.668 from golfshake.com.

green_fee_weekday_thb.note: Inclusive of mandatory caddie per golfdd.com 2023. Older sources (2014) cite 300 THB — likely outdated.

caddie_fee_thb.note: Caddie included in green fee; separate caddie fee not applicable.

prose.overview: Ubolratana Dam Golf Course is an 18-hole, par 72 layout built and operated by the Electricity Generating Authority of Thailand (EGAT) at the Ubolratana Reservoir in Khon Kaen province, approximately 50 km north of Khon Kaen city. Opened in 1986 alongside the dam's operational infrastructure, the course is one of the oldest public-access layouts in Isan and remains open to civilian golfers despite its government ownership. The setting is exceptional by any measure: the course sits in the forested hills surrounding the reservoir, with views across the dam's vast surface — a landscape entirely unlike the flat basin terrain of most northeastern courses. Green fees are all-inclusive at 560 THB weekday (caddie included), making it the best-value full round in Isan for those willing to make the drive. No cart hire is available and there is no driving range.
prose.layout_and_experience:
The Ubolratana Dam Golf Course is defined by its setting more than its design pedigree — EGAT built it to serve its employees and the surrounding community rather than to attract international golf tourism, and the layout reflects that functional origin. What it lacks in branded architectural ambition, it more than compensates for with one of the most distinctive backdrops in Thai golf: the course routes through forested hill terrain above the reservoir, with holes that use natural elevation changes, tree-lined corridors, and the occasional glimpse across the dam itself.

At par 72 across 18 holes, the course presents genuine length and variety. The terrain is not flat — the dam-side topography introduces uphill and downhill tee shots unusual in Isan — and the tree coverage creates a naturally framed playing corridor on most holes. The greens, while not maintained to resort standards, are consistent and hold approach shots reasonably well.

Because no carts are available, walking the course is the only option — which, given the elevation changes, makes this a more physically demanding round than the flat Khon Kaen basin courses. The caddie requirement (included in the green fee, which is also unusual) means golfers are accompanied throughout. The caddie team at a long-established government course like this typically offers reliable local knowledge — club selection and line management on the dam-side holes in particular benefit from familiarity.

Ubolratana Dam is a course for golfers who value setting and character over conditioning and amenity. At 560 THB all-in for 18 holes with a caddie, the value is exceptional; the experience is genuinely memorable.
prose.tips:
Ubolratana Dam Golf Course is approximately 50 km north of Khon Kaen city — about an hour's drive — making it a half-day excursion rather than a city-access course. The all-inclusive green fee (560 THB weekday, 760 THB weekend) makes it the cheapest full 18-hole round with caddie in Isan; no additional caddie fee applies, and no cart is available. This is walk-only golf.

Call ahead to +66 43 372 228 to book a tee time and confirm current fees — the course has no website and listings-site prices may lag real-world pricing by a year or more. The older listing of 300 THB found on some sites is almost certainly outdated.

The Ubolratana Reservoir area is a popular tourist destination in its own right: the dam wall, surrounding forested hills, and on-site accommodation options run by EGAT make this a viable overnight stop. Pairing a round with a visit to the dam infrastructure and a night at the reservoir is one of the more unusual golf trip options in the northeast. The area is best visited in the dry season (November–April) when the reservoir is fuller and the surrounding landscape more dramatic.
prose.location_and_access: Ubolratana Dam Golf Course is located approximately 50 km north of Khon Kaen city in the Ubolratana District, at the Ubolratana Reservoir in Khon Kaen province — around 450 km northeast of Bangkok. The most practical access is from Khon Kaen city by car or Grab: Highway 2 north to the dam area takes approximately one hour. Khon Kaen Airport receives multiple daily flights from Bangkok (under one hour), and onward transport to the dam is by Grab or rental car. There is no direct public transport from Khon Kaen city to the course. Phone: +66 43 372 228.
prose.rental_cta_context: Making the trip to Ubolratana Dam for one of Isan's most scenic rounds? LENGOLF can equip you with premium rental clubs in Bangkok before you head northeast — no need to haul a bag up to the reservoir.
